diffs.diff:
--- a/model.py
+++ b/model.py
@@ -27,7 +27,7 @@
         self.gcn2 = GCNConv(hidden_dim, hidden_dim)

         

         # Simple temporal processing with LSTM

-        self.lstm = nn.LSTM(input_size=hidden_dim, 

+        self.lstm = nn.LSTM(input_size=num_nodes, 

                            hidden_size=hidden_dim, 

                            num_layers=1, 

                            batch_first=True)

@@ -55,22 +55,9 @@
         # x shape: (batch_size, input_seq_len, num_nodes)

         batch_size, input_seq_len, num_nodes = x.shape

         

-        # Process each node through GCN first to capture spatial dependencies

-        # Reshape x to (batch_size * input_seq_len, num_nodes)

-        x_reshaped = x.view(batch_size * input_seq_len, num_nodes)

-        

-        # Apply first GCN layer

-        x_gcn = self.gcn1(x_reshaped, self.edge_index, self.edge_weights)  # Shape: (batch_size * input_seq_len, hidden_dim)

-        x_gcn = F.relu(x_gcn)

-        

-        # Apply second GCN layer

-        x_gcn = self.gcn2(x_gcn, self.edge_index, self.edge_weights)  # Shape: (batch_size * input_seq_len, hidden_dim)

-        

-        # Reshape back to (batch_size, input_seq_len, hidden_dim) for LSTM processing

-        x_lstm = x_gcn.view(batch_size, input_seq_len, self.hidden_dim)

-        

-        # Apply LSTM to capture temporal dependencies

-        lstm_out, _ = self.lstm(x_lstm)  # Shape: (batch_size, input_seq_len, hidden_dim)

+        # Process each time step through LSTM to capture temporal dependencies

+        # x shape: (batch_size, input_seq_len, num_nodes)

+        lstm_out, _ = self.lstm(x)  # Shape: (batch_size, input_seq_len, hidden_dim)

         

         # Take the last output of LSTM for prediction

         last_output = lstm_out[:, -1, :]  # Shape: (batch_size, hidden_dim)
